concerns.text: Task T013 [US2] depends on `data/static_baseline.csv` (producer: T011 in Phase 3). However, T013 is marked `[P]` (parallel-safe). This is a semantic violation: T013 cannot run in parallel with T011 because it consumes the artifact T011 produces. The `[P]` tag is incorrect for T013.

Task T021 [US3] merges `data/static_baseline.csv` (Phase 3) and `data/processed/semantic_results.json` (Phase 4). T021 is marked `[P]`. This is a semantic violation: T021 cannot run in parallel with T019 (producer of semantic_results.json) or T011. The `[P]` tag is incorrect for T021.

Task T006 sets up logging infrastructure for FR-008 (RAM/CPU monitoring). However, the actual implementation of the monitoring logic (the 'producer' of the metrics) is split into T020 (US2) and missing for US1. T006 is a setup task, but the dependency chain for FR-008 is broken: T006 (setup) -> T020 (implementation). T020 is correctly placed in US2, but there is no corresponding task in US1 to implement the monitoring for the static baseline pipeline, creating a gap in the artifact flow for FR-008 compliance.

Tasks T007-T012 are all marked `[P]` or unmarked within Phase 3. T007 (sample data) must precede T008 (metrics) and T009 (pylint), which must precede T011 (CSV write). While they are in the same phase, marking them all as `[P]` implies they can run in parallel, which is false due to data dependencies (T007 -> T008 -> T011). The `[P]` tag on T008, T009, T010, T011, T012 is incorrect.
